title: tephrosia lyallii
intro: Tephrosia lyallii, also known as New Zealand tephrosia or karamu, is a leguminous shrub native to New Zealand, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and oval-shaped leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Tephrosia lyallii include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. The plant can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
